l1: Question 1 — Is the issue affecting one machine or multiple machines across the environment?
Question 2 — What is the OS version and build number of the affected machine?
Question 3 — What specific update (KB number) is failing, or are all updates failing?
Question 4 — What is the exact error code shown in Windows Update settings?
Question 5 — Is this a physical machine, virtual machine, or Azure VM?
Question 6 — Is Windows Update managed via WSUS, SCCM, Intune, or directly from Microsoft?
Question 7 — Has the machine successfully installed any updates recently?
Question 8 — When did the update failures first start occurring?
l2: What do the CBS.log and WindowsUpdate.log show for the failing update?
Has the Windows Update service (wuauserv) and its dependencies been checked and restarted?
Is the SoftwareDistribution folder intact, or does it show signs of corruption?
Are there any pending reboots blocking the update from completing?
Has the DISM and SFC scan been run to check for component store corruption?
Is the machine able to reach the update endpoints (Microsoft/WSUS server) over the network?

l1: Question 1 — What is the exact error code shown (e.g. 0x80070005, 0x800705b4, 0x80240034)?
Question 2 — At what stage does the update fail — downloading, installing, or finalizing?
Question 3 — Does the update fail immediately or after a long wait?
Question 4 — Is there any third-party security or endpoint protection software installed?
Question 5 — Has a manual reboot been attempted since the failure started?
Question 6 — Does the issue reproduce consistently or intermittently?
l2: What specific error and component are referenced in the CBS.log at the time of failure?
Does running the Windows Update Troubleshooter report any issues it could not automatically fix?
Is the BITS (Background Intelligent Transfer Service) running and functional?
Are there any Group Policy settings restricting Windows Update behavior on this machine?
Does the issue occur in a clean boot state with third-party services disabled?
Is the machine's system clock synchronized correctly, as certificate validation depends on accurate time?

l1: Run the Windows Update Troubleshooter from Settings > Update & Security > Troubleshoot.
Stop the wuauserv and bits services, rename the SoftwareDistribution folder, then restart both services.
Run SFC /scannow to repair any corrupted system files.
Ensure the machine has a stable internet connection and can reach update servers.
Check for and remove any conflicting third-party software that may block Windows Update.
Manually download the failing KB from the Microsoft Update Catalog and install offline.
l2: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth to repair the Windows component store.
Analyse CBS.log for the exact failing component and cross-reference with known KB issues.
Check and reset Group Policy settings that may conflict with update delivery.
Verify and repair WMI repository if wuauserv fails to start or enumerate updates.
Review WSUS or SCCM console for approvals, sync errors, or boundary issues if managed.
Use SetupDiag.exe to analyse failed feature update logs and identify the root cause.

l1: Collect the Windows Update log using: Get-WindowsUpdateLog (PowerShell) or %windir%\Logs\WindowsUpdate.
Export CBS.log from %windir%\Logs\CBS\CBS.log.
Collect System and Application event logs in .evtx format.
l2: Collect a full DISM log from %windir%\Logs\DISM\dism.log.
Run and collect TSS for Windows Update scenario (TSS WU).
Collect SetupDiag output if a feature update failed.

update_fails_0x80070005.title: Windows Update fails with error 0x80070005 (Access Denied)

steps.action: Open Settings > Windows Update and note the exact error code and KB number.
steps.expect: Error 0x80070005 (ACCESS DENIED) is confirmed against a specific update.

steps.action: Check CBS.log (%windir%\Logs\CBS\CBS.log) for 'access denied' or 'hr = 0x80070005' entries.
steps.expect: Log confirms a permissions failure during staging or installing the update.

steps.action: Run SFC /scannow in an elevated command prompt.
steps.expect: SFC reports either no violations found or repairs corrupted files successfully.

steps.action: Check if any Group Policy restricts Windows Update permissions (gpedit.msc > Computer Configuration > Administrative Templates > Windows Components > Windows Update).
steps.expect: No restrictive policy should be blocking the update service account.

steps.action: Run DISM /Online /Cleanup-Image /RestoreHealth to repair the component store.
steps.expect: DISM completes successfully and reports the operation completed successfully.

steps.action: Reset Windows Update components: stop wuauserv and bits, rename SoftwareDistribution to SoftwareDistribution.old, then restart both services.
steps.expect: Services restart cleanly and Windows Update re-creates the SoftwareDistribution folder.

steps.action: Retry the Windows Update and monitor the CBS.log in real time.
steps.expect: Update downloads and installs successfully without the 0x80070005 error.
